open_questions: Next ticket — TASK-005 (npm audit, quick), TASK-008 (LOW polish), TASK-001 (wire local task store; the originally-planned next), or pause?
Claude Code CLI auto-update failure reported by the user out-of-band ('✗ Auto-update failed · Try /status'). Investigate manually if it recurs; harness-level, not project-level.
blockers: Atlassian and GitHub MCP credentials not yet set (ATLASSIAN_SITE_URL, ATLASSIAN_USER_EMAIL, ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN, GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN). Blocks TASK-003 only; TASK-004, TASK-001, and TASK-002 can proceed without them.

decisions.decision: TASK-020 spike closed (done, doc committed bd252d3). Minted the 7-ticket plugin packaging chain TASK-021..027 from the spike's §F. Three human decisions locked into the chain: (1) mint all 7 phases; (2) EXCLUDE bin/make-template.js from the shipped plugin bin/ (publish-time dev script only) — baked into TASK-021 AC4; (3) /init-project writes the project CLAUDE.md routing via PROMPT-ONCE-then-marker-merge, never silent/clobber — baked into TASK-025 ACs 1-2. Chain critical path: P1(021)→P2(022)→P3(023)→P4(024)→P5(025)→P7(027); P6(026, MCP) branches off P3 and is the deferrable 'broaden later' seam.
decisions.rationale: Spike delivered all 6 ACs with the retargeting blast radius shown to be small (3 bin/ shells + 1 dep-location bug; all src/ already repoRoot-clean). AC4 proof-of-load executed by the orchestrator as a sensor: validate/install/details/uninstall via a relative-path local marketplace, agent+command registered, zero residue — and the `claude plugin` CLI is non-interactive so it's reusable for chain verification. Two load-bearing findings shaped the chain: (a) a plugin-root CLAUDE.md is NOT loaded as context, forcing orchestrator activation via a generated/merged project CLAUDE.md + backstop skill (TASK-025); (b) agents/orchestrator.md still documents the v1 single-file session model and MUST be reconciled before shipping or every user project inherits the wrong contract (TASK-025 AC4 — flagged by the spike as the highest-risk correctness item). Dev-repo self-use preservation (don't break the agents we build WITH while relocating them to plugin layout) baked into TASK-021 AC5. Deferred publish decisions (marketplace hosting/name, explicit version) parked in TASK-027 to be resolved with implementation context rather than guessed up front. (Extends the 2026-05-28T22:10 plugin-direction decision below.)

decisions.decision: New strategic direction: distribute the framework as a Claude Code PLUGIN (primary vehicle), with a bundled MCP server designed in as the seam to broaden to non-Code clients later. Target surface chosen by the human: 'devs now, broaden later'. Spawned TASK-020 as an architecture SPIKE (not impl) to produce the design + a phased impl ticket chain before any plugin code is written.
decisions.rationale: Verdict was MCP is the WRONG primary vehicle: claude-code-guide confirmed (cited docs) that the Agent-tool subagent orchestration — the framework's core value — is exclusive to Claude Code and CANNOT be carried by an MCP server (subagents are file-based .claude/agents/ constructs). Plugins, by contrast, bundle agents+skills+commands+hooks+MCP as one `/plugin install`-able, versioned, auto-discovered unit — mapping almost exactly onto the human's 'install and go' vision. Corrected two overclaims in the human's framing: there is nothing to 'train' (agents are prompt definitions; agent-generator already CONFIGURES them per project type), and the framework gets a user 'ready to develop anything' (scaffolded + configured + backlog-seeded + orchestrated with human-in-the-loop gates) — NOT a one-shot autonomous product generator, by deliberate design. Sequenced as a spike because repoRoot-retargeting (today everything assumes the framework's own repo is the project; as a plugin it must operate on the user's project) and orchestrator ship/activation in an arbitrary project are genuine unknowns that must be designed before minting impl tickets. Most src/ functions already take repoRoot as a param, so retargeting is tractable repackaging rather than a rewrite — but the CLI shells, state/+tasks/ layout, and CLAUDE.md routing co-location are the open questions the spike resolves.

decisions.decision: TASK-019 closed APPROVE on first review pass (human-authorized close per principle #4). New bin/make-template.js delivers a safe-by-default upstream template-prep command — fixes clone distribution residue at the source rather than leaning on the runtime archiveFrameworkHistory safety net. Spawned from a live end-to-end clone test (clone → npm install → node bin/init.js) that proved the created-branch flow works but leaves two pieces of residue the runtime archiver does not clean: (1) TASK-* .md sidecars survive (archiver regex only matches TASK-NNN.json), (2) the dev session bundle lingers in state/sessions/. Out-of-scope runtime-archiver hardening deliberately NOT folded in — noted in the ticket description for a possible follow-up to keep this ticket single-concern.
decisions.rationale: Cleanest possible review: 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW / 2 cosmetic NITs, 206/206 vitest specs deterministic across two reviewer runs. Two-commit cadence preserved tests-first discipline (0d7ce87 test = 15 failing specs across all 7 ACs; 153a8f0 impl = bin/make-template.js + README 'Preparing a distribution' note; git diff over tests/ between them is empty). Key design wins the reviewer verified: (a) computeTargets() is the single source of truth — the dry-run plan and the apply loop consume the same arrays so the printed plan can never drift from what --yes executes; (b) apply:false performs ZERO filesystem mutation (early return + byte-identity snapshot test), so a careless invocation cannot destroy the live dev repo — the central safety guarantee of a destructive tool; (c) both JSON rewrites are exact-byte via atomicWriteFile. The 2 NITs (TASK_RESIDUE_RE=/^TASK-/ breadth vs the archiver's stricter pattern — safe because tasks/ ships empty; README prose softer than naming base-agent files) deferred without a follow-up ticket per the TASK-013/015/016 close pattern. Net new tests +15 (191 → 206). Also corrected an earlier orchestrator error in-flight: init is NOT runnable on a bare clone (dep chain backlog-seeder → task-store → ajv needs npm install) — now documented in the README via AC7.

decisions.decision: Use a local JSON task store under tasks/ as the interim ticket source before Jira is wired up.
decisions.rationale: Lets the full Orchestrator→Researcher→Developer→Reviewer workflow run end-to-end without external dependencies. Field names mirror Jira so migration is loss-free.

decisions.decision: One JSON file per task plus a regenerable index.json.
decisions.rationale: Cleaner diffs, easier per-task migration to Jira than a single monolithic JSON.

decisions.decision: Persist Orchestrator session state to state/session.json with atomic writes on every meaningful transition; new chats must read it before any other tool call.
decisions.rationale: Lets the human restart the chat without losing context. A single small file plus the active task JSON is enough for a fresh chat to resume.

decisions.decision: Remove the fork-bomb entry from .claude/settings.json permissions.deny.
decisions.rationale: The parser rejected it as empty parentheses, so it was providing zero protection. Other deny rules (git push --force, git reset --hard, rm -rf /) are intact.

decisions.decision: Plan session management as TASK-004 ahead of TASK-001. Augment Claude Code's native transcript handling rather than replace it; introduce portable per-session bundles under state/sessions/<id>/ and a portable knowledge/ lessons-learned KB consulted by the Researcher before web research.
decisions.rationale: User needs cross-machine session portability and cross-project lesson reuse. Building this before the task-store wiring (TASK-001) means every subsequent task immediately benefits from continuity + KB lookups.

decisions.decision: Keep state-of-record as files (JSON + Markdown) rather than migrating to a SQL or NoSQL database for session/KB storage.
decisions.rationale: User proposed a DB-backed store for stability and SQL sort/query benefits. Rejected because the stated #1 requirement was cross-machine portability + inspectability; a directory of JSON+MD wins on zip-and-email portability, git diff visibility, and zero-install inspection. The query-performance gains from SQL don't apply at current scale (0 closed sessions, 0 KB entries). If the KB grows past ~200 entries an opt-in SQLite-as-derived-index can be added later, with the .md files remaining source of truth.

decisions.decision: Resolved all 10 open design questions for TASK-004 (full text in tasks/TASK-004.json comment dated 2026-05-24). Two deviations from researcher recommendations: (a) transcript snapshot taken on every pause + end (not end-only); (b) schema-version field unified as `schema_version` in both pointer and bundle (not split as `version`/`schema_version`).
decisions.rationale: Both deviations prioritize user-stated requirements over researcher's cost concerns. (a) Portability + crash recovery > a small amount of extra I/O. (b) Naming consistency for future readers > a refactor cost that is effectively zero since no production code yet consumes the v1 field name.

decisions.decision: Run liftV1ToV2 on the live state/session.json after the phase-3a code commit (05b2818). The v1 payload is now preserved verbatim inside the new bundle 20260524T000000Z-65fa92c6, and state/session.json is the v2 pointer. From this point forward, all orchestrator state edits go to state/sessions/<id>/session.json, not state/session.json.
decisions.rationale: Phase 3a delivered the lift function but explicitly left the live state untouched (per orchestrator scope contract). Running the lift unlocks test #1 (pointer_v2_shape_minimal) and exercises the migration path end-to-end against real data instead of fixtures. The bundle id YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ format used the v1 updated_at (2026-05-24T00:00:00Z) so chronology is preserved as designed.

decisions.decision: Claude Code transcript directory on Windows confirmed at ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-repo-path>/ where the encoding replaces \, /, : with -. Verified by the Developer against this repo's own transcript files (the framework's directory contains four active jsonl files). Defaulted in src/transcript.js with CLAUDE_CODE_TRANSCRIPT_DIR env-var override and silent no-op when missing.
decisions.rationale: Closes research §I question 3. The encoding scheme matches the same pattern the orchestrator's memory directory uses (`C:\Users\srpar\.claude\projects\C--Users-srpar-OneDrive-Documents-agentic-framework\memory\`), so future projects on this same machine will discover their own transcripts automatically with no configuration.

decisions.decision: TASK-004 implementation is functionally complete; all 30 tests pass on the first run of phase 3b with no test edits and no phase-3a regressions.
decisions.rationale: Two-phase split (3a lifecycle/state-plumbing, 3b KB/summary/inspection/docs) gave clear review boundaries and let the Orchestrator run the live-state lift between phases. Final commit history is 12 commits ending in 0894227. Per CLAUDE.md §Workflow item 6 the next required step is the Reviewer subagent in a fresh context.

decisions.decision: Add `atomicWriteFiles` (two-phase commit batched primitive) to src/atomic-write.js rather than inlining the recipe in src/task-store.js.
decisions.rationale: The TASK-001 atomic-write spec requires `max(fsyncSync.callOrder) < min(renameSync.callOrder)` across the whole mutation (task file + index file). Two sequential single-file `atomicWriteFile` calls interleave fsync/rename, violating the invariant. Two options: (a) add a batched primitive to atomic-write.js, (b) duplicate the EBUSY retry loop + tmp-naming in task-store.js. Picked (a) — additive (no existing single-file caller changes), keeps the EBUSY recipe in one place, and gives any future multi-file writer the same semantics for free. Reviewer audited every existing single-file caller (src/bundle.js ×2, src/knowledge.js, src/pointer.js, src/transcript.js) and confirmed zero regression.

decisions.decision: TASK-001 closed APPROVE on the first review pass. 5 LOWs spun out as TASK-009 (hardening) rather than fixed in-band.
decisions.rationale: Reviewer verdict was clean (0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 5 LOW, 2 NIT) and independently re-validated comment shape via ajv round-trip against tasks/schema.json. All 5 LOWs are real edge cases (partial-commit window, TOCTOU, no tmp recovery for tasks/, listTodos ignores depends_on, no defensive ajv on read) but none block correctness today. Spinning them into a dedicated follow-up keeps the TASK-001 close clean and gives the next operator an unambiguous punch list. TASK-008 (TASK-004 LOWs) and TASK-009 (TASK-001 LOWs) can be batched in a single hardening sprint when the team is ready.

decisions.decision: Orchestrator authorized the impl developer to fix a one-line `import Ajv from 'ajv'` -> `'ajv/dist/2020.js'` test infrastructure bug inside the TASK-002 IMPL commit, overriding the standard 'do not edit tests during impl' rule.
decisions.rationale: Phase-1 dev's `created_task_conforms_to_schema` test imported default Ajv (draft-07-only metaschema) against `tasks/schema.json` which declares draft-2020-12. The test couldn't compile the schema regardless of input data — pure test-infra defect with the production code being correct. Three other test files (live-state, pointer, knowledge-files) use plain Ajv and pass because their target schemas omit `$schema` and Ajv defaults to draft-07 implicitly. Bouncing back to phase-1 dev would have been process theater (same single-line fix either way). Pragmatic call: authorize in-band, require the impl commit body to document the override explicitly so the reviewer can argue it. Reviewer confirmed the documentation, the fix as one line, no assertion weakening, and zero regression in other Ajv consumers.

decisions.decision: TASK-002 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. MEDIUM lexical-sort finding rolled into TASK-009 (AC6) rather than spawning a new ticket.
decisions.rationale: Reviewer flagged that `buildIndexBytes`/`listTodos` use string-compare on the key, so `TASK-1000` lexically sorts before `TASK-999` once a 4-digit key exists. Real bug, but pre-existing from TASK-001 (TASK-002's padding test only checked the new key string, not its index position) and only manifests when a 4-digit key lands in the store — which won't happen for hundreds of tickets. Same module, same fix profile (one-line comparator swap on two lines) as the other 5 task-store hardening items already in TASK-009. Rolling in as AC6 keeps the hardening work in one bucket rather than fragmenting it across two follow-up tickets. Two LOWs (CLI AC-count silent coercion, title-whitespace not trimmed) declined without a follow-up — both are minor ergonomic gaps that don't violate any spec, and `tasks/schema.json`'s title minLength is 1 which already accepts whitespace by design.

decisions.decision: Build a project-intake wizard on top of our own primitives instead of adopting GSD wholesale. Front-load with TASK-010 (question engine).
decisions.rationale: User wants the framework to be cloneable by a non-technical operator who is then walked through an intake that configures agents and seeds a backlog. Two paths existed: (a) adopt GSD `/gsd-new-project` and its `.planning/` model, (b) build our own intake reusing what we already have (prompter from bin/new-task.js, atomicWriteFiles, lifecycle bundles for resumability, task store). User picked (b) explicitly because GSD's `.planning/` collides with our `tasks/` + `state/sessions/` model — two state systems would guarantee 'why doesn't this ticket appear' as the first support bug. Project-type taxonomy locked as (A) curated enum {web-saas, cli-tool, data-pipeline, ml-model, library} + 'other' escape hatch that defers to LLM inference; predictable for non-technical users, flexible when it doesn't fit. Six-ticket chain: TASK-010 (engine, this one), TASK-011 (question library + PROJECT.md), TASK-012 (bin/init.js + CLAUDE.md first-chat routing), TASK-013 (agent generator per project type), TASK-014 (backlog seeder from use cases), TASK-015 (UX polish + non-technical README quickstart).

decisions.decision: TASK-010 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. 3-phase engine invocation in bin/new-task.js. MEDIUM dead-code rolled into TASK-015 (the UX polish ticket that will revisit the same question array).
decisions.rationale: Reviewer in fresh context (independent) approved with 0 HIGH / 1 MEDIUM cosmetic / 7 LOW / 2 NIT. 74/74 specs green twice. The MEDIUM (dead-code `validate` on title question that required:true already covers, plus a typo `engineerPrompter`) lives in the same file TASK-015 will touch for UX-polish anyway, so rolling it forward avoids a one-off polish commit and keeps each follow-up ticket self-contained. Phase-2 dev's IMPL commit body was empty — design decisions captured in the TASK-010 close comment instead so the trail survives. The 4 LOW design questions (engine `when` undefined semantics, silent JSON.parse swallow on corrupt resume, no re-validation of restored answers, three-phase invocation breaks cross-phase backtrack) are all correctly answered at the engine layer; TASK-012 wizard is where strict-resume policy can be opt-in.

decisions.decision: TASK-011 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. Per-branch namespacing applied to ALL id collisions (not just the `language` one phase-1 dev flagged). MEDIUM + 4 LOWs rolled into new TASK-016 (PROJECT.md hardening), to be taken BEFORE TASK-013 lands.
decisions.rationale: Phase-2 dev surfaced and applied namespacing to both `language` -> {cli_language, library_language} AND `deployment_target` -> {web_deployment_target, ml_deployment_target}. Defensible call — keeps prompt wording specific per branch, no shared-question complexity. Reviewer's MEDIUM (comma-in-array-item silent corruption) isn't reachable from the wizard happy path (engine's `multi` type splits on `,` itself) but BECOMES reachable when TASK-013 lands a non-wizard caller of writeProjectMd. Rolling it + the 4 same-module LOWs into a focused hardening ticket (TASK-016) lets us gate TASK-013 on it cleanly. Phase-2 dev kept stack bullets in snake_case (not humanized) — defensible because humanizing+dehumanizing is irreversible for round-trip; TASK-015 can add a separate `label` field per question if pretty labels are wanted. IMPL commit body documented all three orchestrator-required decisions this time (namespacing list, YAML parser subset, BODY_SECTIONS single-source-of-truth) — clean improvement over TASK-010 phase-2 dev's empty body.

decisions.decision: TASK-018 closed APPROVE on first review pass. countFrameworkHistory aligned with backlog-seeder AC3 corruption-propagation policy via option (a). Two doc-polish LOWs from the TASK-017 review landed in the same commit. Last actionable hardening ticket in the backlog — only TASK-003 (Jira mig, MCP-blocked) remains open.
decisions.rationale: Cleanest review of the session: 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW / 1 NIT (anchor-3 comment placement at upper bound of AC4 test window — non-blocking future-proofing concern). 191/191 vitest specs deterministic in 10.18s. Two-commit cadence: 7c400fe (test, 4 failing for the right reasons — three swallow scenarios + one missing-anchor scenario) → 222b285 (impl, +14/-6 in bin/init.js and +9/-5 in src/backlog-seeder.js). The structural fix is the right shape: countFrameworkHistory's throw lands SYNCHRONOUSLY before any await prompter(...) call in maybeArchiveFrameworkHistory, so the half-archive trap is eliminated by construction rather than paper guard. Reviewer's AC4-window NIT documented for the next docstring-wordsmith pass — moving the TASK-017 AC2 reference one line higher would silently break the regression test. Net new tests this session: +36 across TASK-017 + TASK-018 (155 → 191). The framework is now in its cleanest state of the project: every closed ticket reviewer-APPROVED, every active code path tested, 0 npm audit findings, no outstanding hardening polish queue.

decisions.decision: Session sweep: TASK-005 + hardening sprint (TASK-009 + TASK-008 + TASK-017) all closed sequentially. 7 closes total this session. Net new tests +32 (155 → 187). 1 follow-up spawned (TASK-018 for countFrameworkHistory corruption-policy alignment).
decisions.rationale: After the wizard chain landed (TASK-015 done), the user pushed 'keep going' which sequenced through TASK-005 (quick npm audit win via overrides — avoided 2.x→4.x vitest breaking bump), then TASK-009 (7-AC task-store hardening — biggest of the hardening tickets, sets up TASK-017's DRY import via newly-exported TASK_FILENAME_RE), then TASK-008 (5 TASK-004 LOWs — split loadActiveBundle into pause-strict vs end-fallback so error codes are distinct), then TASK-017 (6→7 ACs after the TASK-009 review folded a 7th in mid-flight). Three reviewer-flagged items got new follow-up homes: TASK-009 AC7 absorbed TASK-014 mkdir-wrong-layer MEDIUM, TASK-017 absorbed TASK-009 LOW about readAllTasksSync filter divergence, TASK-018 absorbed TASK-017 MEDIUM about countFrameworkHistory swallow-vs-propagate divergence. Pattern: cross-ticket reviewer findings flow forward into the next-natural-home rather than spawning fragmented one-finding tickets.

decisions.decision: TASK-015 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. Wizard chain milestone — all 6 of 6 tickets complete. Framework now usable end-to-end by a non-technical operator.
decisions.rationale: Sixth and final ticket of the wizard chain. Cleanest reviewer result of the entire chain: 0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 3 LOW, 3 NIT — all polish, none blocking. 155/155 specs deterministic across two clean runs. Two architectural calls vindicated: (a) framework-history detection via inverse-of-`seed`-label heuristic survived all 6 reviewer probes including the corrupt-ticket and pre-existing-archive-clobber edges; (b) archive prompt in `created` branch only (NOT `forced`) is the right cut — separates PROJECT.md regen concerns from task-store mutation concerns. The 3 LOWs (partial-archive rollback, error-message wording divergence, no post-archive confirmation) were deferred without a follow-up ticket per the TASK-013/TASK-016 close pattern — all dormant or cosmetic, none reachable by the happy-path non-technical user. Chain summary: TASK-010 engine → TASK-011 question library + PROJECT.md → TASK-012 bin/init.js entrypoint + CLAUDE.md routing → TASK-016 PROJECT.md hardening gate → TASK-013 agent generator → TASK-014 backlog seeder → TASK-015 README + archive + sweep. 17 net new tests added across the chain (74 → 155). Outstanding hardening tickets (TASK-008/009/017) can be batched in a single sprint when the team is ready.

decisions.decision: TASK-014 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. 3 MEDIUMs split between two hardening tickets: TASK-009 absorbed the mkdir item as AC7; TASK-017 spawned for the 2 seeder-specific MEDIUMs + 3 LOWs + 2 NITs.
decisions.rationale: Reviewer found 3 MEDIUMs — substantial enough that 'defer without ticket' (the TASK-013/TASK-016 close pattern) is too hand-wavy. Split semantically: (a) mkdir-self-bootstrap belongs in src/task-store.js#createTask, which is TASK-009 territory — added as AC7, same pattern as TASK-002's MEDIUM that became TASK-009 AC6; (b) Set-dedup-on-use-cases and CSV-string-silent-zero both live in src/backlog-seeder.js, no existing hardening home, so spawned TASK-017. Two-ticket split keeps each follow-up scoped to one module and one reviewer's worth of context. The TASK-014 reviewer correctly flagged that the wizard's happy path doesn't trip either MEDIUM (engine pre-normalizes input), but a future non-wizard caller of seedBacklog WILL trip them — so neither is dormant in the way the TASK-013 LOWs were. Spawning a dedicated ticket gives the next developer an unambiguous punch list rather than asking them to mine reviewer comments.

decisions.decision: TASK-013 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. Generator wired into resumed branch (not just created/forced per spec) — vindicated by reviewer as defensible NIT. 3 LOW + 4 NIT findings deferred without follow-up ticket.
decisions.rationale: Impl dev shipped a wider hook surface than the ticket required — instead of only `created` and `forced` branches calling generateProjectContext, the shared runWizardAndWriteProjectMd helper covers `created`, `forced`, AND `resumed`. Dev's stated rationale ('completing intake-via-resume produces the same answers map as a fresh created branch — punching a hole there would be surprising') was independently checked by the reviewer who traced every path under which project-context.md could already exist when the resumed branch fires and found no clobber scenario reachable through normal use. The 3 LOWs (generated_at filter symmetry, TYPE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE drift-from-PROJECT_TYPES, unknown-type frontmatter inconsistency) are all dormant or minor — none gate TASK-014 (backlog seeder). Following the TASK-016 close pattern, deferring without a new TASK-018 polish ticket; if a TASK-015 UX-polish sprint materializes later it can sweep these in alongside the LOWs already queued from TASK-010 + TASK-012.

decisions.decision: TASK-016 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. 3 LOW + 2 NIT findings from the reviewer deferred without spawning a follow-up ticket.
decisions.rationale: All 5 ACs verified by independent reviewer (fresh context, 106/106 specs, two clean runs). The 3 LOWs are real but minor: (a) splitInlineArray strips edge whitespace per item, not lossless for items with leading/trailing spaces but writer never emits such items; (b) `formatStackValue` collapses `['']` to `[]` on disk — pre-existing asymmetry, not introduced by this ticket; (c) splitInlineArray comment description slightly off from impl behavior. The 2 NITs (unreachable lone-backslash recovery branch in decoder, test gap on `['\,', ',']` input) are even smaller. None block TASK-013, which was the trigger event TASK-016 was created to address. Spawning a TASK-017 follow-up for purely-polish findings would fragment the project-md hardening work; if they ever matter they can be folded into TASK-015 (UX polish) which already touches similar surface. AC1 escape strategy was the key locked design choice — tests-first dev picked backslash-escape over write-time-reject because the ticket explicitly required 'lossless round-trip for all wizard-generated inputs' and escape strictly dominates reject on that axis.

decisions.decision: TASK-012 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. Dropped `hostname` parameter from runInit entirely after phase-1 dev surfaced a signature gap with startSession.
decisions.rationale: Phase-1 dev correctly flagged that `startSession` calls `hostFingerprint()` directly via `os.hostname()` — there's no plumbing point for an injected hostname. Three options surfaced: (a) extend startSession with optional hostname override (scope creep into src/lifecycle.js), (b) accept hostname in runInit and ignore (ugly), (c) drop hostname from runInit's contract (clean, YAGNI). Picked (c). Tests still pass `hostname: 'test-host'` but never assert on it; JS silently ignores the extra key. Reviewer ran in fresh context, returned 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 1 LOW / 2 NIT — cleanest review of the wizard chain so far. 98/98 specs green across two runs (deterministic). The 1 LOW (positional tokens silently ignored vs bin/new-task.js's strict throw on any unrecognized token) rolled into TASK-015 since the same UX polish pass already covers bin/new-task.js's dead-code validate and engineerPrompter typo. CLAUDE.md amendment placement verified by reviewer's offset check. The framework is now usable by a non-technical operator who clones the repo and runs `node bin/init.js` — wizard chain milestone 3 of 6 complete.

subagent_results.summary: Delivered the full design contract for TASK-004: bundle layout, pointer-file schema with v1→v2 migration rule, Windows-aware atomic-write recipe for lifecycle ops, summary.md content sourcing, inspection commands, KB layout + deterministic lookup procedure, Researcher contract diff, 26 test cases mapped to acceptance criteria including a cross-machine round-trip test, and 10 open design questions in §I. No new tech stack was introduced so no Agent Skill was created.

Proposed Node.js (≥20) + Vitest as the test runtime. Decisive reason: Vitest can mock node:fs at the exact API layer the lifecycle code uses, which is the only way to unit-test the atomic temp+rename call-order invariant that AC4/AC5 hinge on. Surprising finding: the two 'free' Windows-native alternatives are not actually available — Python 3.7.2 on this machine is EOL (since June 2023), and Pester on PowerShell 5.1 is stuck at API-incompatible v3.4.0. So Node was effectively the only ready-to-go option. Approved by human; runtime committed in 9b86b91.

subagent_results.task: TASK-004 failing test suite (phase 2 of tests-first)
subagent_results.summary: Wrote the full failing test suite across 10 spec files mapping 1:1 to research §H's 26 cases plus 4 extras (EBUSY retry, pause-missing-handoff fail-fast, v1-lift refuse-when-non-empty, missing-task-in-destination). Used three helpers — fixtures.js with a frozen PROD map of production import specifiers, tmpRepo.js, repoRoot.js — and five fixtures including a v1 single-file state for migration tests and three KB entries (one valid, two malformed). After Orchestrator unblocked `npm install` (61 packages, gitignored), `npm test` runs in ~1.9s with 30/30 tests failing: 22 MODULE_NOT_FOUND on production imports (no src/ yet), 4 ENOENT on knowledge/ not-yet-created, 4 assertion failures on still-v1 schema and docs. vi.mock('node:fs') factory in atomic-write.spec.js loaded cleanly. Zero edits needed between writing and verifying. Committed in 305ac4f. Implementation phase pending.

subagent_results.task: TASK-004 MEDIUM fixes from Reviewer audit
subagent_results.summary: Fixed both MEDIUM findings. M1: replaced the buggy [A-Z]:\\ regex pattern in both knowledge spec files with a pattern that matches real Windows paths (C:\Users\foo and \\?\C:\Users\foo); added a deny_list_matches_real_paths regression self-test to prove the lint enforces AC8. M2: relaxed state/bundle.schema.json + src/schemas.js — removed maxLength caps on next_action and subagent_results[].summary, declared task and agentId as explicit optional properties on subagent_results items (the rigorous path; rejected additionalProperties:true so future drift still gets flagged). Added tests/live-state.spec.js that validates the live pointer + bundle session.json against their schemas on every test run, with full ajv errors dumped on failure. npm test now reports 32 passed / 0 failed / 32 total in ~5s; previous 30 still pass with zero regressions. Committed in d196308.

Wrote 11 failing specs across 4 describe blocks (AC1 listTodos ×3, AC2 transitionStatus ×4, AC3 appendComment ×3, ATOMIC ×1). Added one entry to the frozen PROD map in tests/helpers/fixtures.js — taskStore: prodUrl('task-store.js') — matching the file:// URL pattern used by the other 10 modules. Created 4 fixture tasks (TASK-101..104) covering all 5 statuses including one with a seed comment for the preserve-comments test. After npm test: 11 new failures (all `Failed to load url .../src/task-store.js` — Vite's MODULE_NOT_FOUND surface — the right reason) + 32 pre-existing pass. Caught one orchestrator-introduced regression on the side: workflow_step:'tests_first' was not in the bundle schema enum [idle|fetch|research|test|impl|review|update]; flagged in the punch list, Orchestrator fixed pre-impl. Commit 5619336.

Implemented src/task-store.js with the three exports the suite pins (listTodos, transitionStatus, appendComment), each routed through atomic writes and each regenerating tasks/index.json after a mutation. Hard-coded STATUSES constant mirroring tasks/schema.json with a one-line comment pointing at the schema as source of truth. Key design move: added a new additive export `atomicWriteFiles` (two-phase commit) to src/atomic-write.js to satisfy the writes_go_through_atomic_write spec's invariant max(fsync.callOrder) < min(rename.callOrder). Two-phase recipe: open+write+fsync+close ALL tmps first, then rename ALL. Shares the same EBUSY/EPERM retry loop as the single-file primitive. Both mutators (transitionStatus, appendComment) now read all per-task files once, mutate in-memory, then batch-write [task.json, index.json] together — the index payload reflects the same snapshot as the task write. After npm test: 43/43 pass in ~3.3s, no regressions to any of the 32 pre-existing specs. Commit 9bfb956.

Wrote 15 failing specs across two new files: 11 for the createTask core (key derivation, schema conformance via ajv, default application, input validation, atomic write ordering) + 4 for the runCli CLI wrapper (flag parsing, interactive prompts, empty-title re-prompt, flag+prompt mix). Added one PROD-map entry `newTaskCli` via a new `binUrl` helper that mirrors the existing `prodUrl` pattern. Created TASK-999 fixture for the 999->1000 padding-boundary test. Picked option (a) for createTask location: stays inside src/task-store.js as a single new export rather than spinning a new src/new-task.js — rationale: only in-repo caller is the CLI itself, so promoting readAllTasks/buildIndexBytes/tasksDir to public exports just to feed one neighbor file is unjustified API widening. After npm test: 15 new failures (11 `TypeError: createTask is not a function`, 4 `Failed to load url .../bin/new-task.js`) + 43 pre-existing pass. Commit 675294e.

subagent_results.task: TASK-002 implementation (impl phase, with one authorized test edit)
subagent_results.summary: Implemented `createTask` + `deriveNextKey` privately inside src/task-store.js (no helper promotion, per phase-1 decision). Added bin/new-task.js with `runCli` export, injectable prompter, `node:readline/promises` for the real TTY path, and `pathToFileURL`-based executable detection so Windows backslashes don't break the gate. Initially blocked on a test failure: `created_task_conforms_to_schema` couldn't compile `tasks/schema.json` because the test imported default Ajv (draft-07) but the schema declares draft-2020-12. Surfaced the blocker rather than editing the test unilaterally; orchestrator authorized a one-line import swap to `ajv/dist/2020.js` and required the impl commit body to document the override. Commit 00e7153 (3 files, 224 insertions). All 58 specs pass in ~3.8s. Orchestrator-run shell smoke from /tmp produced a valid TASK-001.json with all schema-required fields and identical created_at/updated_at timestamps. Surfaced one out-of-scope concern: buildIndexBytes uses lexical sort on the full key string, so TASK-1000 collates before TASK-999 — pre-existing from TASK-001; rolled into TASK-009 AC6.

Verdict: **APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS**. 0 HIGH, 1 MEDIUM, 2 LOW, 1 NIT. Independently re-ran npm test from clean state: 58/58 pass, ~3.6s, no flakes. Independently ajv2020-validated a createTask output against tasks/schema.json: VALID:true, all 8 required fields present, no additionalProperties leaks, comments/jira_key/assignee defaults correct. Verified the authorized one-line test edit is genuinely one line, doesn't weaken assertions, the schema-conformance test now actually validates the on-disk file, and other Ajv consumers (live-state, pointer, knowledge-files) still pass. Confirmed validation precedes disk I/O in createTask (line 190-199 before 223-227), single `now()` call reused for both timestamps, single net-new export (createTask), Windows-safe `pathToFileURL` for executable detection, single atomicWriteFiles call (not two sequential atomicWriteFile). The MEDIUM finding — lexical sort in buildIndexBytes/listTodos mis-orders TASK-1000 against TASK-999 — is real but pre-existing from TASK-001 and only manifests at 4-digit keys; rolled into TASK-009 AC6. The 2 LOWs (CLI AC-count silent coercion to 1, title-whitespace not trimmed) are minor ergonomic gaps deferred without follow-up since the schema already accepts whitespace via minLength:1.

Verdict: **APPROVE**. 0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 5 LOW, 2 NIT. Independently re-ran npm test from clean state twice (3.20s then 3.35s — stable, no flake). Independently round-tripped an appendComment-produced payload through ajv 2020 + ajv-formats against tasks/schema.json — VALID:true, comment shape exactly {author,at,body}. Verified all 4 ACs mapped to named tests with concrete on-disk assertions. Confirmed atomicWriteFiles is genuinely two-phase (all fsyncs before any rename) and additive — read all 4 existing single-file callers (src/bundle.js ×2, src/knowledge.js, src/pointer.js, src/transcript.js) and confirmed zero regression risk. Confirmed error paths: invalid status throws before reading disk; unknown key throws after read but before any write; both leave on-disk bytes byte-identical. Determinism: injected `now` is honored byte-for-byte in both per-task file and index.generated_at; no Date.now() leak. The 5 LOWs (partial-commit window in atomicWriteFiles, TOCTOU between readAllTasks and write, no tmp recovery sweeper for tasks/, listTodos ignores depends_on, no defensive ajv on read) carried over to TASK-009. The 2 NITs (unreachable `throw lastErr` in both atomic-write loops, hand-maintained STATUSES enum) flagged as harmless and left as-is.

Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. Independently re-ran npm test from clean state — 30/30 pass in 4.05s (verbose 4.45s), no console warnings, no flaky behavior. 0 HIGH findings (would have blocked), 2 MEDIUM, 5 LOW, 1 NIT. Reviewer explicitly noted: 'if the team prefers strict-clean-on-merge then the schema drift in particular deserves a HIGH reclassification.' MEDIUM #1: tests/knowledge-files.spec.js + tests/knowledge-lookup.spec.js Windows-path deny-list regex compiles to '[A-Z]:\\' which requires literal double-backslash; a real Windows path C:\foo would NOT trip the lint, silently weakening AC8. MEDIUM #2: live state/sessions/<id>/session.json does not validate against state/bundle.schema.json — next_action exceeds maxLength:300, subagent_results items contain task and agentId which violate additionalProperties:false, and summary strings exceed maxLength:1000. Tests don't catch this because they use small synthetic bundles via fixtures.js. The 5 LOWs (researcher Write scope, ambiguous error in loadActiveBundleWithFallback, non-atomic summary.md write, comment drift in migrate.js, too-loose regex in lifecycle test #174) were captured in TASK-008. Full coverage map and resolved-questions implementation check confirm all 12 ACs and all 10 resolved questions correctly implemented.

subagent_results.task: TASK-004 phase 3b implementation (knowledge base, summary, inspection, doc migrations)
subagent_results.summary: Implemented the remaining 13 tests on the first run with no test edits. New: src/summary.js (full summary.md generator per research §D, computes active/paused durations from lifecycle.log, sources commits from each touched task's linked_commits), src/inspection.js (listSessions sorted newest-first + showSession returning structured payload; strictly read-only), src/knowledge.js (validateEntry + lookupKnowledge with deterministic three-pass token scoring tag*3+symptom*2+body*1 + recordKbReuse atomic last_seen_at update). knowledge/ bootstrap with README + schema.md + schema.json + two seed entries (the *.log gitignore lesson and the Windows atomic-rename recipe). CLAUDE.md, state/README.md, .claude/agents/researcher.md all migrated to v2 layout. Phase-3a cosmetics fixed: $id formatting restored, lifecycle.endSession now calls generateSummary with noop-on-already-ended preserved, dual-axis schema_version dimensions documented in src/schemas.js. **Transcript path discovered**: ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-repo-path>/ with backslash/slash/colon -> dash encoding, verified against this repo's live jsonl files. Final test count 30 passed / 0 failed / 30 total in ~3s. Committed 0894227. Zero scope leak, no installs.

subagent_results.task: TASK-004 phase 3a implementation (lifecycle, atomic-write, bundle, pointer-v2)
subagent_results.summary: Implemented the lifecycle/state-plumbing half of TASK-004. 9 src/ modules cover atomic temp+rename with EBUSY/EPERM retry, orphan-tmp recovery sweep, bundle directory layout with YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ-<8hex> ids, v2 pointer read/write, the v1→v2 lift refusing re-lift when state/sessions/ non-empty (Q #10), CLAUDE_CODE_TRANSCRIPT_DIR env-var-driven transcript snapshot copier (Q #3 punted), lifecycle ops with idempotent noops and snapshot-on-every-pause+end (Q #2), pending_human_confirmation preserved across pause (Q #7), and SHA-256-hostname fingerprint (Q #9). state/session.schema.json rewritten to v2 pointer shape; state/bundle.schema.json added. Three test edits — over-eager writeFileSync trap removed, async expect for lift, PROD map switched to file:// URLs computed from import.meta.url for Vite resolver compatibility. After commit (05b2818) and the orchestrator-run live-state lift, npm test reports 17 passed / 13 failed / 30 total — exactly the phase-3a/3b split. Three cosmetic concerns flagged for phase 3b: $id formatting glitch in src/schemas.js, placeholder summary.md write on end (3b must replace), dual schema_version dimensions (pointer/bundle vs manifest bundle-layout).

next_action: TASK-021 (P1 scaffold) IN PROGRESS — tests-first phase. Both chain risks pre-handled: (R2) CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR confirmed UNSET in Bash-tool env but cwd==project root works — recorded in TASK-022 for P2 (cwd fallback + SessionStart hook). (R1) human chose 'keep both + drift-guard' for agent relocation — .claude/agents/ stays live (this session keeps spawning subagents), plugin agents/ gets copies, tests/agents-parity.spec.js guards drift. Repo-local scope confirmed: 4 agents + 1 skill (tech-training-template); global gsd-* skills are NOT repo-local, do not sweep. Developer spawned for failing tests. After green-failing: impl, then review. Open backlog: TASK-021 (active), TASK-022..027, TASK-003 (MCP-blocked).
handoff_summary: 🎉 **TASK-018 closed APPROVE — backlog drained, only TASK-003 (Jira mig, MCP-blocked) remains open.** Cleanest review of the entire session: 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW / 1 NIT (AC4 anchor-3 placement, deferred). 191/191 vitest specs deterministic in 10.18s across 30 files. Linked commits: 7c400fe (test) + 222b285 (impl). Two structural wins: (1) `bin/init.js#countFrameworkHistory` no longer silently swallows JSON.parse failures — corruption now propagates with the offending filename BEFORE any `await prompter(...)` call in `maybeArchiveFrameworkHistory`, eliminating the half-archive trap by construction rather than paper guard; (2) the `src/backlog-seeder.js#readAllTasksSync` docstring is post-AC7-accurate (drops `"Duplicated intentionally"`, cites TASK-017 AC7 / TASK_FILENAME_RE DRY-import) and the unknown-use-case-slug comment now references TASK-017 AC2's design lock so future maintainers don't read it as an oversight. Session totals: 8 tickets closed (TASK-016/013/014/015/005/009/008/017/018), 0 open hardening items remain, 0 npm audit findings, +36 net new tests this session (155 → 191). Sixth and final ticket of the six-ticket chain (TASK-010..015) done. Four deliverables landed in this ticket: (1) `README.md` at repo root (152 lines, no internal jargon, walks a non-technical operator from clone to first chat with Claude Code); (2) new `src/framework-history.js` exporting `archiveFrameworkHistory({repoRoot, now})` that detects pre-existing framework tickets via the inverse of the `seed` label and moves them to `.framework-history/tasks/` atomically via `renameSync`, regenerating `tasks/index.json` empty; (3) `bin/init.js` archive prompt in the `created` branch only — default-Y on empty input, `--no-archive` flag suppresses prompt, `forced`/`resumed`/`already_initialized` correctly skip; (4) accumulated punch-list sweep — `bin/new-task.js` typo `engineerPrompter` → `enginePrompter` corrected, dead-code `validate` on title question removed (ac_count's validate preserved as load-bearing), `bin/init.js` argv parser now throws on stray positional tokens matching `bin/new-task.js`'s strictness. The 3 LOWs are polish (partial-archive rollback, error-message wording divergence vs bin/new-task.js, no on-screen post-archive confirmation) — none block anything, deferred without follow-up ticket per the TASK-013/TASK-016 close pattern. **WIZARD CHAIN STATUS — ALL 6 OF 6 DONE:** TASK-010 (engine), TASK-011 (library + PROJECT.md), TASK-012 (init + routing), TASK-016 (project-md hardening, gating), TASK-013 (agent generator), TASK-014 (backlog seeder), TASK-015 (UX polish — this one). New module `src/backlog-seeder.js` exports `seedBacklog({repoRoot, answers, now})` returning `{created: <string[]>}`. Module declares `USE_CASE_TEMPLATES` (frozen, 6 keys matching primary_use_cases enum — data-entry/reporting/integration/automation/collaboration have 2+ templates each, other has 1) and `COMMON_TEMPLATES` (single 'Set up project CI' ticket emitted on every fresh seed). Each minted ticket gets `labels: ['seed', ...template.labels]` (Set-deduped) plus an opening comment from author `'backlog-seeder'` whose body names the use case or the literal string `'common'`. Idempotency guard reads tasks/ on entry and short-circuits to `{created: []}` if any existing ticket carries the `seed` label — so `--force` re-runs don't duplicate or clobber. Dev added `mkdirSync(tasks/)` as a workaround for fresh-repo ENOENT (startSession doesn't create tasks/); reviewer flagged this as architecturally misplaced (belongs in createTask itself) and orchestrator rolled the fix into TASK-009 as AC7. `bin/init.js` calls seedBacklog after generateProjectContext succeeds via the shared runWizardAndWriteProjectMd helper (created/forced/resumed); already_initialized correctly skipped. New module `src/agent-generator.js` exports `generateProjectContext({repoRoot, answers, now})` which writes `.claude/agents/project-context.md` via atomicWriteFile. When `answers` is omitted the generator reads PROJECT.md via readProjectMd; when provided it skips the read entirely. Generated file has YAML frontmatter (project_name, project_type, generated_at, schema_version:1) and four body sections in locked order: `## Stack`, `## Testing conventions`, `## Linting and formatting`, `## Type-specific guidance`. Per-type guidance is a single `TYPE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE` module-top map keyed by each of the six PROJECT_TYPES (web-saas/cli-tool/data-pipeline/ml-model/library/other) with 4 substantive bullets per type matching the test's per-type keyword whitelist. `bin/init.js` calls the generator after writeProjectMd succeeds — dev wired it into the shared runWizardAndWriteProjectMd helper which runs in `created`, `forced`, AND `resumed` branches; reviewer independently traced clobber scenarios and confirmed no path reachable through normal use, so the extension is a NIT not a defect. `already_initialized` branch does NOT regenerate (sentinel test in tests/agent-generator.spec.js confirms). All four base agent files (developer.md, reviewer.md, researcher.md, orchestrator.md) gained a single sentence in their Inputs section instructing the agent to read .claude/agents/project-context.md before starting work, with a 'may not exist on fresh clones — proceed with defaults' caveat. Reviewer in fresh context: APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS (0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 3 LOW, 4 NIT). 123/123 vitest specs deterministic across two runs (~4.8s). The 3 LOWs are polish: (a) ROUNDTRIP_NOISE_IDS missing `generated_at` filter symmetry (dormant), (b) TYPE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE keys not import-derived from PROJECT_TYPES so taxonomy widening silently falls back to 'other' bullets, (c) unknown project_type values emit typo verbatim in frontmatter while body falls back to 'other'. Five fixes landed in a single impl commit (b9147a6): (1) AC1 lossless comma escape in inline arrays via backslash encoding — `\` escaped first then `,` as `\,`, decoded with single left-to-right scan in new helpers `encodeArrayItem`/`decodeArrayItem`/`splitInlineArray`; affects formatStackValue (writer) and both reader call sites (coerceFrontmatterScalar + parseStackValue inline-array branches). (2) AC2 schema-aware scalar coercion — `state/PROJECT.schema.json` readFileSync-d once at module load with try/catch graceful-degrade to empty schema; `coerceFrontmatterScalar(raw, fieldName)` now coerces numeric-looking values to Number only when schema.properties[fieldName].type is integer or number. (3) AC3 inline comment on parseStackValue documenting the deliberate string-only handling for unknown Stack keys. (4) AC4 writeProjectMd throws BEFORE any disk I/O when project_name or project_type is missing/empty/null; error names the snake_case field. (5) AC5 misleading colon comment rewritten to scope the constraint to frontmatter only.
